VAL-SCENE-001.evidence: Test 'copy_front_to_back_preserves_nodes_and_data' at scene.rs:1849 (labeled VAL-SCENE-001): builds a scene with root node (1024x768, bg Color::rgb(30,30,30)) and child node with mono text data 'Hello, world!', swaps to front, calls copy_front_to_back(), then byte-compares all front nodes against back nodes via repr(C) pointer cast, and asserts front_data == back_data. All node bytes match after copy. Parent validator confirmed 1489 tests pass with 0 failures.

VAL-SCENE-002.evidence: Test 'mark_changed_records_node_ids' at scene.rs:1972 (labeled VAL-SCENE-002): allocates 8 nodes, swaps, copies forward, marks nodes 3 (clock) and 7 (cursor) as changed, swaps again, then reads change_list via DoubleReader. Asserts change_list has length 2, entries are [3, 7], and is_full_repaint() is false. Confirms change list records exactly the marked node IDs. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass.

VAL-SCENE-003.evidence: Test 'double_reader_reads_change_list_from_front' at scene.rs:2009 (labeled VAL-SCENE-003): builds scene, swaps, copies forward, mutates node 0's background and marks it changed, swaps. Creates DoubleReader on same buffer, asserts is_full_repaint() is false, change_list().unwrap() has len 1 and entry [0]. Confirms compositor-side DoubleReader can read writer-produced change list from the front buffer. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass.

VAL-SCENE-004.evidence: Test 'copy_front_to_back_resets_change_list' at scene.rs:1941 (labeled VAL-SCENE-004): marks node 0 as changed, swaps, then calls copy_front_to_back(). Reads change_list from the new back buffer via DoubleReader — asserts change_list().unwrap().len() == 0 (empty after copy). Previous frame's changes do not carry over. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass.

VAL-SCENE-005.evidence: Test 'update_data_in_place_after_copy_forward' at scene.rs:2319 (labeled VAL-SCENE-005): pushes clock data b'12:34:56', swaps, copies forward, then calls update_data(clock_dref, b'12:35:00') — asserts true (same length, succeeds). Calls update_data(clock_dref, b'ABC') — asserts false (wrong length, fails). After swap, DoubleReader reads data at clock_dref and asserts it equals b'12:35:00'. Additionally, pre-existing tests 'writer_update_data_in_place' (line 665) and 'writer_update_data_wrong_length_fails' (line 675) cover basic update_data behavior. Parent confirmed 1489 tests pass.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. Each assertion has a dedicated, labeled unit test in scene.rs that exercises the exact behavior described in the validation contract.
